requirements.summary: Fix member login authentication flow to properly validate membership IDs and display specific error messages instead of generic 'failed' status
acceptanceCriteria: Members can successfully log in using their membership ID
Login errors display specific, actionable error messages instead of generic 'failed' status
The useMemberAuth hook properly validates membership IDs against the backend
The MemberLoginPage component handles authentication errors gracefully with clear user feedback

file_operations.description: Enhance the login function in useMemberAuth to call the backend validateMemberLogin method with proper error handling. Add detailed console logging to capture validation responses, network errors, and authentication state changes. Parse backend responses to extract specific error types (invalid ID, expired membership, inactive member) and store them in state. Ensure localStorage and cross-tab synchronization logic remains unchanged per constraints.

Update error display logic to show specific, user-friendly error messages based on the error type returned from useMemberAuth. Add distinct error states for: invalid membership ID, expired membership, inactive member account, and network/server errors. Include actionable guidance in error messages (e.g., 'Contact the gym desk to renew your membership' for expired memberships). Preserve existing retry logic and query cache clearing behavior.

Add a new React Query mutation hook useMemberLoginMutation that wraps the backend memberLogin method with comprehensive error logging. Capture and log request parameters, response data, response timing, and any errors. Return structured error information including error type, message, and original backend response for debugging. Ensure the hook integrates with existing query invalidation patterns.

requirements.summary: Add comprehensive error logging and diagnostics to the member authentication flow to capture detailed failure information
acceptanceCriteria: Console logs show detailed error information when member login fails
Backend validateMemberLogin function returns specific error codes for different failure scenarios (invalid ID, expired membership, etc.)
Frontend displays user-friendly error messages based on specific failure reasons

file_operations.description: Add structured console logging throughout the authentication flow capturing: login attempt timestamp, membership ID (sanitized/masked for security), backend validation request/response, localStorage operations, and authentication state transitions. Log error stack traces and network error details. Include log levels (info, warn, error) to distinguish between normal flow and failures. Add a unique session/attempt ID to correlate related log entries.

Add console logging for user interaction events including: login form submission, input validation, error display triggers, and retry attempts. Log the full error object structure received from useMemberAuth to help diagnose error mapping issues. Include timestamp and attempt count in logs for debugging repeated failures.

Create a dedicated logging utility for member authentication with functions to log structured events including loginAttempt, validationRequest, validationResponse, validationError, localStorageOperation, and crossTabSync. Each log entry should include timestamp, attempt ID, membership ID (masked), event type, and relevant data. Export a singleton logger instance configured with appropriate log levels for development vs production.
